filled_slots.build_depth: Agent-first executive intelligence workflow with governed local workspace, mandatory review-only controls, handoff bundle contract, backend validation/handoff helpers, and optional read-only SENTRY review surface. No SQLite persistence, scheduler, publication, or outbound delivery.
filled_slots.source_boundary: Any open-source data that can be located, using OSINT best practices and verification before use. Competitor pricing, assortment, offerings, bypass behavior, and private/personally invasive collection are prohibited.
filled_slots.travel_boundary: Business travel details only when publicly sourced and business-relevant; no home/private tracking or real-time/current-location stalking.
filled_slots.first_output_surface: Local organized data folder under data/executive-intel/ with git-ignore protections.
filled_slots.agent_architecture: Standalone exec-signal-scout agent first; SENTRY is optional downstream consumer of finalized handoff bundles.
filled_slots.usable_workflow: CLI-enabled agent-first workflow: target template generation, portfolio readiness inspection, draft handoff preview, and finalized local handoff export after analyst approval.

assumptions: Public-source does not automatically mean safe for CSO-facing use; signals require verification and analyst review.
Daily full web crawling is not recommended initially; daily delta watch plus weekly analyst synthesis is safer.

RAG is available through exec-signal-scout/walmart-est-mcp context checks for competitor-source summaries; direct helper runtime remains dependency-pending due missing rank_bm25 in the attempted local runtime.

A first supervised public read-only collection pass has been completed for the user-provided Amazon security executive example; artifacts are stored only in the git-ignored data/executive-intel workspace.
Backend prototype includes models, source policy validation, normalization, dedupe, and targeted tests only.
Agent manifest now includes Code Puppy web tools: ScoutBridge via invoke_agent, Goose broker public extraction, built-in browser tools, and Skyvern via MCP relay when allowed.
Smoke test passed after absolute SENTRY paths were added to agent instructions.
First collection artifact validation passed after normalizing citation fields; profile and 10 signals now validate against backend ExecutiveProfile/ExecutiveSignal contracts.

Second supervised corroboration pass completed for Stephen Schmidt/Amazon. It created local git-ignored artifacts, corroborated Chief Security Officer title, found no public support for SVP qualifier, and added 3 contract-valid corroboration signals.

Read-only RAG context check completed through exec-signal-scout with source_filter=Competitor; returned Amazon/Schmidt competitor context from approved local RAG sources without web browsing or file writes.
Read-only SENTRY integration now exposes Executive Signal Scout artifacts as target portfolios and draft reports through /api/executive-intel/* endpoints.
Executive Intel frontend page was added under Intelligence to review profiles, source counts, signal validation, CSO-ready candidates, policy summary, and latest draft brief.
Local smoke test against Stephen Schmidt artifacts returned 1 portfolio, 13 sources, 13 signals, 1 CSO-ready candidate, 0 invalid signals, and NOT_PUBLISHED_REVIEW_REQUIRED report status.

Mandatory review-only controls are now codified as enforcement rules: no SQLite writes, no artifact mutation, no scheduled collection, no report publication, no outbound delivery, no private/current location tracking, no auth/paywall/CAPTCHA bypass, and no competitor pricing/assortment/offering scraping.

The controls explicitly allow compliant review-only work to continue: reviewing approved inputs, parsing artifacts, extracting intelligence, classifying findings, scoring confidence/risk, dedupe, stale-info detection, source traceability, and draft-only summaries.
Executable backend guard added at backend/executive_intel/review_controls.py and surfaced through /api/executive-intel/health.
exec-signal-scout repo manifest and installed local Code Puppy manifest were patched to treat review-only controls as mandatory operating rules.
User clarified SENTRY should not necessarily be the workbench; preferred model is standalone agent producing finalized data for the SENTRY program.
Agent-first handoff contract added: exec-signal-scout -> local review-only workspace -> analyst finalization -> handoff bundle -> optional SENTRY consumption.
Backend handoff builder added at backend/executive_intel/handoff.py; default includes only VERIFIED signals approved for SENTRY/CSO draft, with draft dry-run mode clearly marked review-only.
Read-only smoke test against Stephen Schmidt artifacts built an in-memory DRAFT_HANDOFF_REVIEW_ONLY bundle with 13 total signals, 1 handoff signal, and 8 controls enforced.
Added backend/executive_intel/cli.py with target-template, portfolio, and handoff commands.
Added config/executive_intel_target.example.json as a single-target starter template.
Validated CLI against Stephen Schmidt artifacts: 13 signals, 13 sources, 0 invalid, 1 draft handoff signal.
Handoff file output is explicit-only via --output and refuses overwrite unless --force.
approval_points: Backend prototype implementation was approved by user with 'proceed'. Approval still required before expanding beyond local prototype.
Approval required before browser collection against target executives or scheduled runs.
Approval required before sending, sharing, publishing, or promoting CSO-facing output.
Approval required before changing the current read-only API/UI into SQLite persistence, analyst-edit workflow, promotion queue, scheduled collection, or CSO-facing publication.
Review-only controls are mandatory: if a task mixes permitted and prohibited actions, block only the prohibited action and continue permitted review-only portions.
confirmation_points: User selected both SENTRY workflow profile and standalone manifest.
User requested organized local data folder.
open_questions: Which executive should be used for the next supervised collection run?
Should competitor-owned corporate/newsroom/event pages be default-allowed if they pass source policy, or manually allowlisted per competitor?
What retention period should apply to rejected/private/prohibited leads beyond minimal rejection metadata?
